extraction.one_line_summary: VAN AERT Wout soloed from the final climb 12km out, held off the peloton by 10 seconds in yellow jersey.
tactical_patterns: VAN AERT attacked from yellow on Cote du Cap Blanc Nez climb with 12km remaining, using time trial power to hold off sprinters.
LAPORTE Christophe led out peloton despite teammate VAN AERT being clear, suggesting team prioritized green jersey points harvesting.
POGAČAR lost 18 seconds to yellow jersey on flat stage with categorized climb, revealing GC teams misjudged threat level.

rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: soloed to victory from 12km out in yellow jersey, demonstrating capacity to attack from marked position and sustain time trial effort
rider_signals.LAPORTE Christophe: positive: executed leadout for peloton sprint despite teammate away, indicating tactical flexibility for points competition
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: negative: lost 18 seconds on relatively minor climb finale, now 32 seconds behind, suggesting team underestimated stage danger
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: negative: also dropped 18 seconds on punchy climb finish, now 40-41 seconds back, exposing GC vulnerability on non-mountain stages
rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: neutral: appeared to contest sprint finish for second place behind VAN AERT
rider_signals.CORT Magnus: neutral: attempted early breakaway for KOM points, maintaining pattern from Denmark stages
rider_signals.PEREZ Anthony: neutral: joined early KOM break with CORT, targeting polka dots after 2020 attempt ended in crash
